response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable senior software engineer with broad full-stack and systems experience, but his background is almost entirely in web, mobile, and application software development. The Principal Performance Architect role demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, GPU architecture, and deep-learning workload analysis — none of which appear in his resume. His closest hardware-adjacent experience is writing C/C++ components for a measurement IDE at National Instruments and working with robotic arms at Altom, neither of which bridges to the chip-level performance architecture this role requires.
strengths: Over 15 years of software engineering experience, satisfying the Bachelor's + 8-year education threshold
Demonstrated low-level performance work in high-throughput environments using DLang async I/O and caching for millions of requests per second
Some hardware-adjacent exposure: C/C++ instrumentation work at National Instruments and robotic arm development
concerns: No evidence of SoC or IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or chip-level architecture work
No AI, deep learning, GPU, or AI accelerator experience of any kind — the core domain of this role
Career trajectory is firmly in web/full-stack software; no signal of transition toward or interest in hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No AI/deep learning or GPU workload expertise
Low-level systems and performance experience (DLang, C/C++)
